benchmark_details.name: AUTOHALLUSION
benchmark_details.overview: AUTOHALLUSION is the first automated benchmark generation approach that employs several key strategies to create a diverse range of hallucination examples for evaluating large vision-language models (LVLMs), which are susceptible to hallucinations. This benchmark allows the investigation of common failure patterns and reasons for hallucinations in LVLMs.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: The primary objective of the benchmark is to create a diverse set of hallucination examples that can better evaluate the reliability of LVLMs and aid in understanding their hallucination mechanisms.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: The dataset has limitations in terms of diversity of questions, as they mainly focus on object existence and spatial relations without exploring attributes like color and pattern.

data.source: Images were generated using image generation models like DALL-E-3, alongside existing datasets such as the Common Objects in Context (COCO) dataset.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are calculated based on comparing generated responses of LVLMs with ground truth based on hallucination scenarios.
methodology.interpretation: Higher rates of ASR, MASR, and CASR suggest better hallucination induction and detection capabilities of the LVLMs.
methodology.validation: Extensive evaluations conducted across top-tier LVLMs including GPT-4V(ision), Gemini Pro Vision, Claude 3, and LLaV A-1.5.

harm: Potential for inducing misinformation through hallucinations.
